code_scope.implementation_note: This is an EL specification. Current codebase (Nimbus) is CL client and does not implement EL precompiles.

summary: Ceil vs floor division in word calculation causes consensus split for non-multiple-of-8 input lengths.

phase1_abstract_interpretation.summary: Floor division produces smaller word counts for max_length % 8 != 0, causing gas cost divergence. State anomalies: underflow in word count, gas cost divergence, consensus split potential.
state_anomalies_found: Underflow in word count when using floor division
Gas cost divergence proportional to words^2 difference
Consensus split potential between clients with different rounding

phase2_symbolic_execution.summary: Counterexample found: any max_length where max_length % 8 != 0 triggers divergence between ceil and floor implementations.

counterexample.path_conditions: PC1: max_length > 32, PC2: max_length % 8 != 0, PC3: Implementation uses floor division
counterexample.expected_outcome: Clients using ceil produce gas_cost with complexity=50, clients using floor produce complexity=32, causing consensus failure on transaction validation.

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.summary: Attacker fully controls base_length and modulus_length via transaction calldata. Single transaction with non-multiple-of-8 length triggers immediate consensus split.
phase2_5_reachability_analysis.data_flow_path: Transaction calldata → ModExp precompile → Parse lengths (attacker-controlled) → Compute max_length → Compute words (ceil vs floor) → Gas calculation → Consensus validation → Split if implementations differ

validation_layers.effectiveness: Does not validate precompile input alignment

phase2_5_reachability_analysis.notes: No validation layer prevents attacker from submitting transaction with max_length % 8 != 0. Bug triggers on every such transaction.

phase3_invariant_proving.summary: Cannot prove invariant (words = ceil(max_length / 8)) due to counterexample in Phase 2. Specification explicitly requires ceil, but incorrect implementation using floor violates invariant for all non-multiple-of-8 lengths.
phase3_invariant_proving.violation_condition: max_length % 8 != 0 AND implementation uses floor division
phase3_invariant_proving.critical_finding: No runtime guard validates implementation correctness. Pure implementation bug with guaranteed consensus failure if any client uses floor instead of ceil.

phase3_5_scope_filtering.reason: EIP-7883 is in Fusaka scope, pure EL bug (no API/config/CL dependency), exploitable via on-chain transaction
phase3_5_scope_filtering.recommendation: CRITICAL: Audit ALL EL client implementations of EIP-7883 immediately. Verify each uses ceil(max_length / 8). Add consensus test vectors with lengths 33, 65, 129, 2049.
phase3_5_scope_filtering.severity: HIGH (assumes minority implementation bug) or CRITICAL (if multiple major clients affected)
phase3_5_scope_filtering.notes: This is a specification implementation correctness check. Any client implementing floor instead of ceil will cause immediate consensus split on first non-aligned ModExp transaction post-Fusaka.

metadata.implementation_status: Specification audit (EL client implementations not in current codebase)
